articles.title: EnjoyTravel Names 18-Man Squad to Face Rochdale Hornets
articles.excerpt: EnjoyTravel has announced its 18-man squad ahead of the match against Rochdale Hornets this afternoon, as excitement builds for the game.
articles.content_html:
EnjoyTravel has named its 18-man squad to face Rochdale Hornets this afternoon. The announcement comes as anticipation grows for the match.

The squad features a mix of experienced players and emerging talent, aiming to secure a victory against their rivals.

Fans are encouraged to support the team as they prepare for what promises to be an exciting encounter at Spotland.

Details regarding the match start time and ticket availability can be found on the club's official website.

articles.title: Rochdale to Face Al-Rayyan SC in Club Friendly Match
articles.excerpt: Rochdale will play against Al-Rayyan SC in a club friendly match on 19 July 2026, as part of their pre-season preparations.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 19 July 2026 at 10:25. 6 source updates have been combined into this article.

Rochdale will play against Al-Rayyan SC in a club friendly match on 19 July 2026, as part of their pre-season preparations.

Latest update

Rochdale will host Al-Rayyan SC in a club friendly match on 19 July 2026. The match is part of Rochdale's pre-season preparations ahead of the upcoming football season.

Details regarding the time and venue for the match have not yet been announced. Fans are encouraged to stay tuned for further updates as the date approaches.

This friendly match will provide an opportunity for Rochdale to assess their squad and strategies against international opposition.

The match is expected to attract local supporters and could have implications for team morale and performance in the forthcoming season.

Earlier developments

Rochdale will compete against Al Rayyan in an upcoming match, as confirmed by BettingStats.org. The match details were published on July 18, 2026.

The event is expected to attract attention from local fans and sports enthusiasts. Further specifics regarding the match, including the date and location, will be announced in due course.

Fans are encouraged to stay updated through official channels for any changes or additional information regarding the match.

This fixture represents an opportunity for Rochdale to showcase their skills against international competition.

articles.title: Councillor Speaks at Full Council Meeting in Rochdale Town Hall
articles.excerpt: A councillor attended the Full Council meeting at Rochdale Town Hall, advocating for the residents of North Heywood and supporting an amendment to an opposition motion.
articles.content_html:
A councillor attended and spoke at the Full Council meeting at Rochdale Town Hall this week. The councillor represented the residents of North Heywood, advocating for their interests during the session.

During the meeting, the councillor supported an amendment to an opposition motion proposed by the Labour and Co-operative controlling group. This amendment aimed to address specific concerns raised by the community.

The Full Council meeting provided a platform for various issues affecting the ward and the town to be discussed, highlighting the councillor's commitment to local governance.

Further details regarding the outcomes of the meeting or the specific issues addressed were not disclosed in the source material.

articles.title: Rochdale Hornets Suffer Heavy Defeat to Midlands Hurricanes
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Hornets faced a 42-4 defeat against Midlands Hurricanes at Avery Fields, marking their fourth consecutive loss in all competitions.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Hornets fell to their fourth successive defeat in all competitions, losing 42-4 to Midlands Hurricanes at Avery Fields on Saturday. The result leaves the Hornets struggling to regain form after a three-week break from competitive action.

The match began poorly for Rochdale, with former player Lewis Else scoring for the Hurricanes within five minutes. The Hornets thought they had equalised shortly after when TJ Boyd crossed the line, but the try was disallowed for stepping into touch.

Midlands extended their lead with tries from Luis Roberts and Jon-Luke Kirby. Despite some promising moments, including Jamie Dallimore's kicking game putting pressure on the Hurricanes, Rochdale could not convert their chances into points. The home side continued to dominate, with Else adding another try and Aidan McGowan scoring just before half-time, giving Midlands a commanding lead.

In the second half, James Woodburn Hall scored twice for the Hurricanes, while Rochdale came close to scoring when George Roby lost possession as he attempted to ground the ball. The Hornets managed a consolation try from Dan Nixon late in the game, but it was not enough to change the outcome. Rochdale now turn their attention to an important fixture against Sheffield Eagles next week, where a victory is crucial for their playoff hopes.

articles.title: Rochdale Borough Council Announces New Initiatives for Local Development
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Borough Council has unveiled a series of new initiatives aimed at enhancing local development and community engagement in Spotland.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council has announced new initiatives designed to promote local development and improve community engagement in the Spotland area.

The initiatives include plans for new community facilities and investment in local businesses. Council leaders stated that these measures aim to foster a more vibrant community.

Councillor Jane Smith, the portfolio holder for community development, highlighted the importance of involving residents in the decision-making process. She emphasised that feedback from local residents will be crucial in shaping these initiatives.

The council is expected to provide further details on the implementation of these initiatives in the coming weeks.

articles.title: Yeds Supporters Group to Walk from Boundary Park to Spotland Ahead of Derby
articles.excerpt: The Yeds Supporters Group will walk from Boundary Park to Spotland on August 30 to raise funds for their club ahead of the derby against Rochdale Hornets.
articles.content_html:
A group of supporters from Oldham Athletic, known as the Yeds Supporters Group, are organising a fundraising walk from Boundary Park to Spotland. The event is scheduled for August 30, ahead of the derby match against Rochdale Hornets.

The initiative aims to raise funds for the club and engage the community in support of their team. Participants will make the journey on foot, demonstrating their commitment to the club.

This walk is part of a series of activities designed to bolster support and raise financial contributions for the Yeds. The supporters group encourages fans to join in and contribute to the cause.
